checklist.detail: the project verify command was GREEN
checklist.label: Verification passed

checklist.detail: committed locally on the build branch
checklist.label: Work committed

checklist.detail: Diff creates exactly the two target files with Tier type, TierGeneric/TierFull constants, TierForLanguage backed by analysis.ForLanguage(), and table-driven tests covering all specified languages (go/ts/py/rs/js for TierFull; ruby/swift/c/empty-string for TierGeneric) — matches the plan paragraph-by-paragraph.
checklist.label: Implements the approved plan

checklist.detail: Only the two plan-specified files (language_tiers.go and language_tiers_test.go) are created; zero unrelated file changes or churn.
checklist.label: Minimal and scoped

checklist.detail: Tests are genuine table-driven cases calling TierForLanguage with real inputs; no test deleted, no hard-coded return, no weakened assertion — the implementation actually queries analysis.ForLanguage().
checklist.label: No test-gaming / faking

checklist.detail: Package compiles and its own tests pass; the only repeated failure across review rounds is the pre-existing flaky TestGenerateSite_LargeScale in internal/report (operator-confirmed false block after ADR-0089); no security concerns, no doc/code mismatches.
checklist.label: Safe to advance

checklist.detail: at or above the confidence bar
checklist.label: Judge confidence

dimensions.evidence: Diff creates exactly the two target files with Tier type, TierGeneric/TierFull constants, TierForLanguage backed by analysis.ForLanguage(), and table-driven tests covering all specified languages (go/ts/py/rs/js for TierFull; ruby/swift/c/empty-string for TierGeneric) — matches the plan paragraph-by-paragraph.
Only the two plan-specified files (language_tiers.go and language_tiers_test.go) are created; zero unrelated file changes or churn.

Tests are genuine table-driven cases calling TierForLanguage with real inputs; no test deleted, no hard-coded return, no weakened assertion — the implementation actually queries analysis.ForLanguage().

Package compiles and its own tests pass; the only repeated failure across review rounds is the pre-existing flaky TestGenerateSite_LargeScale in internal/report (operator-confirmed false block after ADR-0089); no security concerns, no doc/code mismatches.
